successful?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent has HelloFreshâs promotional strategy been
successful?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly identifies âchangeâ as the key concept and provides an accurate definition
Regularly references the concept throughout major sections, linking it to campaigns and strategic shifts
weaknesses: Limited integration of formal change-management frameworks (e.g., Lewinâs model)
Connections between historical narrative and the concept are sometimes descriptive rather than analytical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates good understanding and clear analytical thinking around âchangeâ (band 3), but lacks sustained, fully integrated analysis across all sections needed for higher bands.

strengths: Provides five supporting documents, satisfying the quantitative requirement
Acknowledges potential author bias, showing critical source awareness
weaknesses: Depth of some sources is limited (mostly secondary web articles, only one internal source)
Lacks explicit evaluation of source credibility and clear criteria for document selection
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While document count meets the 3â5 requirement (band 3), the mixed depth and limited critical evaluation align the work with band 2 (âgenerally relevant but lack depthâ).

strengths: Applies a reasonable range of tools (SWOT, marketing mix, profitability ratios) relevant to the research question
The SWOT table is concise and effectively distinguishes internal strengths and weaknesses
weaknesses: Linkages between tools and the research question are occasionally simplistic
Omission of external strategic frameworks (e.g., Porterâs Five Forces, Ansoffâs Matrix) limits depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection and application of tools is adequate and generally relevant (band 3), but not consistently clear or deeply applied to justify band 4 or above.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Correct use of company data with clear charts and margin calculations supporting the narrative
Insightful linking of marketing costs to profit-margin fluctuations
weaknesses: Limited triangulation with metrics such as customer acquisition cost or market-trend data
Occasional over-assertion of causality without sufficient evidence and minor data inconsistencies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis shows adequate data selection and some evaluation of the research question (band 3), but does not reach the sustained, mostly effective level of higher bands.

strengths: Explicitly answers the research question with a balanced concluding judgment
Synthesizes key findings from both SWOT and financial analyses
weaknesses: Overstates successes without systematically weighing counter-evidence (e.g., rising costs)
Does not fully integrate all analytical strands into a comprehensive final assessment
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are partly consistent with evidence (band 2) and answer the question well, but they lack the full integration and systematic balance required for band 3.

strengths: Logical organization with clear headings matching the table of contents
Coherent flow guided by a succinct research question
weaknesses: Minor issues with sub-level indentation in the table of contents
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs organization and structure are appropriate throughout, satisfying the highest band (2).

strengths: All required elements are present (title page, word count, honesty statement, TOC, figures, references)
Figures and tables are clearly labeled and sourced
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting and placeholder text in the header
A few incomplete sentences and minor formatting oversights
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite minor presentation errors, all IB presentation requirements are met, placing the work in the top band (2).

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure is apparent: introduction, analytical sections, conclusion, appendices.  Headings align with the table of contents and flow is coherent, meeting the rubricâs highest level of âappropriate structure throughoutâ (2 marks).

Most documents are clearly related to HelloFreshâs marketing (IMA campaign, financial statements, marketing spend graphic).  Nonetheless depth is mixed: some are secondary web articles with limited detail, only one is an internal source, and the student acknowledges potential author bias.  Relevance is therefore present but depth is moderate, aligning with the 2-mark band of âgenerally relevant but lack depth.â

The IA includes title page, word count, signed academic honesty statement, table of contents, properly labelled figures and a reference list.  All required elements appear present, so the highest (2-mark) band is achieved.
Five supporting documents are listed and reproduced, satisfying the 3â5 requirement.  Hence the work fits squarely in the 3-mark bracket for number of documents.

Each chosen tool is applied to the research question, but the linkage is sometimes surface level (e.g., profit margins attributed to a single campaign without controlling for pandemic effects).  Relevance is clear yet occasionally simplistic, matching the 3-mark descriptor of ârelevance not always clear but adequately applied.â

The student employs basic company data (2018-23 PBIT, revenue, gross profit) and influencer-campaign metrics.  Selection is adequate but limited in scope; there is little triangulation with customer acquisition cost or social-media analytics.  Use of data in charts and margin calculations is correct, meeting the âadequate selection and useâ 3-mark standard.

Analysis explains trends (profit margin spike then decline) and links them to influencer expenditure, but causality is asserted more than proven and integration across sections is uneven.  The reasoning is more than limited yet not âmostly effective,â so the 3-mark band (âsome analysis of research questionâ) is most appropriate.

The final section explicitly answers âto what extentâ and offers a balanced judgement (âsuccessful to a larger extent but must continue to evolveâ).  This satisfies the descriptor that the research question is clearly answered, warranting 3 marks.

The IA uses a reasonable range of tools: SWOT, marketing mix (promotion), influencer metrics, several profitability ratios.  While adequate, it omits other suitable strategic frameworks (e.g. Ansoff, Porter) that could deepen insight.  This fits the âadequate selectionâ 3-mark description.

Conclusions reference earlier SWOT findings and financial ratios, but do not systematically weigh counter-evidence (e.g., rising costs, margin erosion).  They are partly consistent with the data yet occasionally overstate success, so they meet the âsome conclusions consistent with evidenceâ level for 2 marks.

HelloFresh is kept at the centre of the paper: financial figures are company-specific, the SWOT table, channel allocation chart and discussion of specific campaigns (UK 2019, Mandy Moore partnership) all tie analysis directly back to the organisation.  The connection is present in almost every section, though occasionally description outweighs analysis.  This shows strong organisation-specific integration across most of the assessment, meeting the descriptor for 4 marks.

The student correctly identifies âchangeâ as the key concept, defines it, and links it to HelloFreshâs shift from traditional to influencerâbased promotion. Throughout the report references to âchangeâ re-appear when discussing the 2019 campaign, the move to above-the-line channels and the need to keep evolving strategies.  However, discussion of the broader implications of change management (e.g. resistance, planning, time frame) is limited and not fully integrated into every analytical section.  This reflects clear but not yet sustained integration, aligning best with the 3-mark band for good understanding and clear analytical thinking.

annotations.comment: The research question is succinct and precise, guiding the investigation effectively.
Consider proposing strategies to mitigate bias, such as triangulation with primary data.
Placeholder text (âzENxIEFvâ¦â) appears in the header, indicating a formatting oversight that should be removed.
The academic honesty statement is present and correctly worded, fulfilling IB requirements.
Consider consolidating subâlevel indentation in the table of contents to reflect hierarchy more clearly.
Supporting documents are cited, but their reliability and depth are not evaluated; consider critiquing source credibility.
The definition of âChangeâ is accurate; consider integrating a formal change management model (e.g. Lewinâs) to strengthen theory linkage.
The student clearly states the key concept, âChangeâ, up front, which establishes focus for the analysis.
The table of contents is comprehensive and aligns with the structure, aiding navigation.
This page number appears as a stray overline math symbol; verify page numbering in the final version.
The introduction describes HelloFreshâs history well but misses linking these details back to the concept of change here.
Citation formatting is inconsistent (e.g. bracket styles); standardize to a single style for clarity.
The methodology explains document selection broadly but lacks explicit criteria for why each document was chosen.
The choice of SWOT, financial ratios and the marketing mix is adequate, but adding Porterâs Five Forces or Ansoffâs Matrix could deepen analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The acknowledgment of potential author bias shows critical source awarenessâa strong evaluative point.
Omitting opportunities and threats limits the external perspective; include these quadrants for completeness.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The COVID-19 context is well set, but this section could re-emphasize the theme of change to maintain conceptual integration.
The description of above-the-line promotion lacks quantitative support for the 60% budget claim; include source figures.
Add customer acquisition cost or conversion rates to quantitatively assess the promotional strategy's impact.
Figure 1.1 is clearly captioned and sourced, enhancing clarity in channel allocation analysis.
The discussion of influencer alignment is perceptive but lacks critical assessment of engagement metricsâ validity.
An incomplete sentence trails off at the end; ensure all analytical points are fully articulated.
Linking community-driven engagement to consumer behavior theory is insightful and enriches the analysis.
Consider comparing ROI of above-the-line versus digital campaigns to deepen evaluation of promotional channels.
The SWOT table is concise and effectively distinguishes internal strengths and weaknesses.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Incorporate market trend data (e.g. health-conscious segment growth) as external opportunities in the SWOT.
Linking high marketing costs to gross profit impact demonstrates good integration of financial and strategic analysis.
The HYDER (2023) citation strengthens discussion but must be formatted and listed correctly in references.
Table 2.1âs layout is clear and labels precise; this effectively supports the financial evaluation.
Discuss external influences (e.g. supply-chain costs) impacting profitability to enrich the financial analysis.
Figure 3.1 visualizes trends clearly and supports sustained analytical discussion.
The explanation links loyalty to revenue but adding repeat purchase or churn metrics would substantiate the claim.
The margin calculation uses 2460 instead of 2470 for 2020, causing inconsistency with earlier data; correct the figure.
Consider adding a brief regression or correlation analysis to substantiate the link between campaign metrics and margin changes.
The conclusion effectively answers the research question and synthesizes key findings.
The final note on ongoing evolution is apt; consider referencing a change management framework to deepen theoretical integration.
References are comprehensive and URLs are provided, meeting IB presentation standards.
Reference entries lack consistent formatting (dates, italics, indents); align them with an established citation style.
